places.name: Vampire Café
places.description: A taste of the macabre Tokyo's ritzy shopping district.

places.name: The Giant Ghibli Clock
places.description: Tokyo’s only Vaudeville-cuckoo clock–steampunk-Victorian curio cabinet time piece.

places.name: Omoide Yokocho
places.description: Colloquially known as “piss alley,” this narrow street filled with tiny bars and barbecue stands looks like it was pulled straight out of “Blade Runner.”

places.name: Gotokuji Temple
places.description: The legendary birthplace of the Japanese maneki-neko is dotted with hundreds of lucky beckoning cats.

places.name: Sanrio Puroland
places.description: If Hello Kitty were Mickey Mouse, this would be Disneyland.

places.name: Godzilla Head
places.description: Not quite the menace it once was, this monster still has some chops.

places.name: Sengaku-Ji
places.description: This Buddhist temple is home to the graves of the Japanese national legends, the 47 Ronin.

places.name: Vampire Café
places.description: A taste of the macabre Tokyo's ritzy shopping district.

places.name: Omoide Yokocho
places.description: Colloquially known as “piss alley,” this narrow street filled with tiny bars and barbecue stands looks like it was pulled straight out of “Blade Runner.”

places.name: 8bit Cafe
places.description: Transport yourself to the 1980s era of video games at this themed restaurant and bar.
